en: Well, well, well, well, welcome back, my friends.
ko: 자, 다시 돌아왔습니다.

en: Ladies and gentlemen, my first guest tonight is an Emmy award-winning comedian you know from her stand-up specials and shows like Curb Your Enthusiasm and The Upshaws.
ko: 신사 숙녀 여러분, 오늘 첫 게스트는 에미상 수상 코미디언 완다 사이크스입니다.

en: She's now making her dramatic debut in the new film, Undercard. Please welcome back to The Late Show, Wanda Sykes.
ko: 새 영화 언더카드로 드라마 데뷔합니다. 완다 사이크스를 환영해주세요.

en: Oh, boy. Wow. They are lovely. People enjoy their Wanda Sykes.
ko: 와. 정말 좋아하네. 완다 사이크스를 즐기시는군요.

en: Thank you. Good to see you. Thank you. Yeah. Hey, how's it going? Haven't seen you since last year.
ko: 감사합니다. 반가워요. 잘 지내요? 작년 이후로 못 봤네.

en: Nice to have you back. How are you doing? Oh, go birds. Somebody said go birds. All right. Go birds.
ko: 반가워요. 어떻게 지내요? 고 버즈. 누가 고 버즈라고 했네.

en: I meant to say that. How am I doing? I'm doing OK. Good. I want to know, how are you doing, man?
ko: 그 말 하려고 했어. 어떻게 지내냐고? 괜찮아. 당신은 어떻게 지내요?

en: I'm doing OK. We're having a good time. OK. I want to know, what's the plan?
ko: 괜찮아요. 즐거운 시간 보내고 있어요. 좋아. 계획이 뭐야?

en: And when I'm asking you this, I'm really asking you, how is Evie doing? How is she doing?
ko: 이걸 물으면서 사실은, 에비는 어때요?

en: Because I know you see the anxiety in her. Yes.
ko: 그녀의 불안감이 보이잖아요.

en: Because she's sitting there going, he's going to be home all the time, trying out his little jokes.
ko: 맨날 집에서 쪼그만 농담 연습하겠네, 라고 앉아서 생각하겠지.

en: She's worried. She has said to me, keep in mind, I have heard all of your stories. OK. That's a good woman.
ko: 걱정하고 있어요. 당신 이야기 다 들었다고 했대요. 좋은 아내네.

en: Because that's what interns are for. Every three months, a new group of people to tell your stories to. No, she's a little worried. Yeah.
ko: 인턴이 그래서 있는 거잖아. 3개월마다 새 사람들한테 이야기해. 좀 걱정하더라.

en: OK. We'll figure it out, though. So the last show, what you going to do? You got to go to hell off the last show.
ko: 알겠어. 어떻게든 해결하지. 마지막 방송은 어떻게 할 거야? 확 날려야지.

en: Like, burn this bitch down. You got to go. OK. I mean. I love this theater, though.
ko: 여길 불태워. 확 가야 해. 근데 이 극장 좋아해.

en: I don't want to. I mean. It's a lovely theater. Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. Sure. Yeah. I heard they turned it into a Walmart.
ko: 안 그래도. 월마트로 바뀐다던데 걱정 마.

en: So don't worry about it. Yeah. Yeah. I mean, you always bring the fire every night.
ko: 매일 밤 불을 지피잖아요.

en: But that last show has to be like destruction. Like, yeah. I'm a lover, not a fighter, Wanda.
ko: 근데 마지막은 파괴적이어야 해. 나는 싸움꾼이 아니라 사랑꾼이야, 완다.

en: See, I knew you was going to say that. All right. OK. And we know. I know who you are as far as you're a man of faith and all that.
ko: 그럴 줄 알았어. 알겠어. 당신이 신앙인이라는 거 알아.

en: So. And love is stronger than hate. So maybe your last guess.
ko: 그리고 사랑이 증오보다 강하지. 그러면 마지막 게스트로.

en: Your last guess. You bring on the Pope. Bring on the Pope. Do you have a Pope connection?
ko: 마지막 게스트로 교황을 불러. 교황 인맥 있어?

en: Because I need a Pope connection. I want to get Leo on here. He's ghosting me. He's holy ghosting me.
ko: 교황 인맥이 필요해. 레오를 데려오고 싶어. 무시당하고 있어. 성령으로 무시당해.

en: OK.   Work on him.
ko: 교황한테 작업해봐.

en: Let's work on him. I go direct. I go direct. Oh, you go right around the Pope. Save the big guy.
ko: 작업하자. 나는 직접 가. 교황을 건너뛰어. 큰 분을 아끼고.

en: I go direct. I should have done that. Yeah. That's the Catholic problem. We got to go through the intermediary. Yeah. I'm going to put that in my prayers tonight.
ko: 직접 가. 그랬어야 했어. 그게 가톨릭 문제야. 중개자를 거쳐야 해. 오늘 기도에 넣을게.

en: Please do. You get the Pope here. But when the Pope gets here, he's got to go to hell off. He got to burn this bitch down.
ko: 부탁해. 교황이 오면 확 날려야 해. 불태워야 해.

en: One of the things I'm looking forward to not doing so much is following the news quite so tightly all the time. I still care, but not have to ingest it all the time.
ko: 안 하고 싶은 것 중 하나가 뉴스를 그렇게 꼼꼼히 안 따라가는 거야. 관심은 있지만 매일 소화할 필요는 없어.

en: Do you follow the news? Are you aware that the president took us into an optional war with Iran? I've heard.
ko: 뉴스 따라가요? 대통령이 이란과 선택적 전쟁을 시작한 거 알아요? 들었어.

en: I've heard. It's ridiculous. It really is. It's unjustified.
ko: 들었어. 터무니없어. 정당하지 않아.

en: And here's the thing. We're going to this war, and it's like, I don't know how many billion dollars a day. One billion a day.
ko: 근데 전쟁에 하루 수십억 달러인데. 하루 10억이야.

en: We're in the middle of a shutdown. We got a shutdown going on, right? And they spend all this money on war.
ko: 셧다운 중이잖아. 전쟁에 이 돈을 쓰면서.

en: And because, like, ooh, we're afraid Iran's going to bomb. I'm not worried about them and their bombs and bombing us.
ko: 이란 폭탄이 무섭다고? 나는 월급 못 받는 TSA 직원이 더 무서워.

en: I'm more scared about these TSA agents not being paid. I don't know. I don't know if you've ever been.
ko: 빅 셜리한테 몸수색 당해본 적 있어? 빅 셜리는 거칠어.

en: See, I don't know if you've ever been pat down by Big Shirley. But Big Shirley's rough.
ko: 빅 셜리가 월급을 못 받으면 손톱 관리도 못 해서 울퉁불퉁한 손톱으로 스웨터를 잡아당기고 긁어.

en: Now, Big Shirley not getting paid, and she's not getting her nails done, so her nails are all raggedy, and she's patting you down, snagging your sweaters, scratching you up.
ko: 안 돼. 이 사람들한테 월급 줘. 하루 10억이라면서.

en: That's a no. Pay these people. Pay these people. Well, you know, you mentioned it's a billion dollars a day.
ko: 맞아. 최소 10억. 정부 재정 운용이 걱정돼?

en: Yeah, yeah. It's at least a billion dollars a day. Yeah. And so you concerned about the fiscal wisdom of how our government spends money?
ko: 당연히. 트럼프가 물가를 낮추겠다고 했는데 더 올랐어.

en: Absolutely. And, you know, and I thought, you know, Trump ran on, oh, I'm a lower prices. Things are going to be so low, going to be so cheap.
ko: 마트에서 뭘 보고 이게 공항에서 더 싸겠다 싶을 정도야.

en: And now prices are even higher. I mean, you know it's bad when you go to the grocery store and you look at something.
ko: 허드슨 뉴스로 갈게. 국방부의 낭비 봤어?

en: You're like, damn, I can get this cheaper at the airport. The freshest vegetables.
ko: 네. 헤그세스가 뭘 샀어? 게 다리.

en: I'm taking my business to the Hudson News. I don't know about this. Did you see the sort of the extravagance that the Pentagon has spent?
ko: 960억 달러를 낭비했어. 게 다리, 스테이크, 랍스터.

en: Yes. Yes. I mean, what? Hagsaf, they bought what? Crab legs.
ko: 전쟁 장관이 아니라 레드 랍스터 장관이야.

en: I mean, it was like $96 billion they wasted. Or like crab legs, steaks, lobsters.
ko: 말도 안 돼. 체다 베이 전투.

en: You know, he's supposed to be Secretary of War. I think he's Secretary of Red Lobster. That's what I'm going to call him now.
ko: 게 다리는 좋아. 나도 좋아.

en: It don't make any sense. The Battle of Cheddar Bay. There you go.
ko: 지금부터 그렇게 부를 거야. 게 다리 헤그세스. 이름이야.

en: Yeah. I like a crab leg, though. I love a crab leg. I love a crab leg, too.
ko: 갑각류 장관. 잠깐 쉬고 완다 사이크스와 돌아옵니다.

en: But that's what I'm calling him now. Crab legs Hagsaf. That's his name. Crab legs.
ko: 곧 돌아옵니다.

en: The Secretary of Crustaceans. We have to take a quick break. We'll be right back with more Wanda Sykes, everybody.
Stick around. We'll be right back.
ko: 곧 돌아옵니다.
